title: mjst config
description:
Generate TypeScript parsers and type definitions from JSON Schemas, with optional README generation.

You can supply options via CLI flags or a JSON config file. CLI flags always take precedence over config file values.
$comment: Supply options either via CLI flags or a JSON config file — both approaches are shown below.

Badge.title: Badge
Badge.description: A shields.io badge rendered in the README header.

label.description: Left-hand label text of the badge.

message.description: Right-hand message text of the badge.

color.description: Hex color (without #) or named color for the message half of the badge.

logo.description: Simple Icons slug for an icon shown on the left of the badge.

logoColor.description: Color of the logo. Only applied when logo is set.

link.description: URL the badge links to when clicked.

InstallStep.title: InstallStep
InstallStep.description: A single installation step rendered as an optional heading followed by a code block.

title.description: Optional heading shown above the code block.

command.description: Shell command displayed inside the code block.

language.description: Syntax-highlighting language identifier for the fenced code block.

UsageExample.title: UsageExample
UsageExample.description: A named usage example rendered as a heading, optional description, and code block.

command.description: Command or code snippet displayed inside the code block.

language.description: Syntax-highlighting language identifier for the fenced code block.

description.description: Explanatory text rendered below the code block.
examples: Generates both runtime parsers and TypeScript type definitions.

schema.description: Path to the JSON Schema file to process. The schema is read, parsed, and used to generate TypeScript source files.

outDir.description: Output directory for generated TypeScript files. The directory is created automatically if it does not exist. Subdirectories are created as needed when a generated file includes a nested path.

typesOnly.description: Generate only TypeScript type definitions without parser functions. Useful when you only need the type shapes and do not need runtime validation.

config.description: Path to a JSON config file. Keys match the option names in this schema (schema, outDir, typesOnly). CLI flags take precedence over config file values.

installation.description:
Controls the Installation section of the generated README.

Provide one or more ordered steps — each renders as an optional heading and a fenced code block. Omit this property entirely to fall back to the auto-detected package manager install command.

steps.description: Ordered installation steps. Each step becomes a code block under the Installation heading.

notes.description: Optional markdown text rendered after all installation steps. Use this for prerequisites, platform notes, or post-install instructions.

badges.description: Extra shields.io badges appended after the auto-generated version, license, and TypeScript badges in the README header.

usage.description:
Controls the Usage section of the generated README.

Provide named examples — each renders as a heading, optional description, and fenced code block. Omit this property to auto-derive an example from the required schema properties that carry x-cli-flag annotations.

examples.description: Ordered usage examples shown under the Usage heading.

notes.description: Optional markdown text rendered after all usage examples.
examples: Run `mjst --help` to see all available flags.

examples.title: Types only — skip parser functions
examples.description: Emits only TypeScript type definitions without runtime validation code.

usage.notes: Run `mjst --help` to see all available flags.

x-example-titles: Full — with installation and usage documentation
